What we help with › Stiffness & limited movement
For all • All ages • Every stage of life

Stiffness & limited movement

Stiffness creeps up on people: a neck that will not turn as far, a back that protests at bending, shoulders and hips that feel tight and reluctant. It is one of the most common things we see, at every age, and it responds well to the right help.

Understanding it · unique copy per page: what it feels like, why it happens in plain terms, reassurance · MUST include the areas-and-causes passage naming the joints, tissues (muscle, fascia, tendon, ligament) and causes (sport, accident, trauma, fall, gradual onset, underlying condition) relevant to this theme; this is the SEO body-coverage layer · sidebar stays verbatim
Understanding it

Why movement gets restricted, in plain terms

You might notice it first thing in the morning, after sitting, or when you try something you have not done for a while: reaching a high shelf, checking a blind spot, getting down to the floor and up again. Restriction like this usually means muscles and joints have tightened, become guarded, or simply lost the habit of moving through their full range. Often the nervous system itself has become hyper-vigilant and guards the body, holding it in postural restriction long after the original problem has passed. It is rarely permanent, and it usually improves once the body is shown, gently and repeatedly, that the movement is safe.

We help with stiff necks and shoulders (including frozen shoulder), backs that have lost their bend, tight hips and hamstrings, and knees and ankles that no longer move freely. The restriction may sit in the muscles, fascia, tendons, ligaments or the joints themselves, and it may follow an injury or operation that healed tight, arthritis or another underlying condition, years of desk work or driving, a spell of illness or inactivity, or simple gradual change over time. Assessment works out where the restriction actually lives and why it is there, and care is then shaped to free it: hands-on treatment, guided movement, and a pace that suits you.

Get in touch

The easiest first step is a conversation. Tell us what is going on and we will point you the right way, with no pressure at all.

Call 07773 814488
Three journey tiles · reframed from service benefits to the visitor's journey · no adjective rotation on condition pages · carries class "expect-navy" as the rhythm-break fallback because condition pages build copy-first; when the veil photo is chosen, remove "expect-navy", uncomment the veil section below and append its per-page CSS rule at the marked slot (180px canon, both simulations)
What to expect

From first call to feeling better

What is likely going on

Stiffness usually means tissues have tightened or become protective, not that a joint is worn out. Restriction that built up over time can very often be unwound the same way.

How we assess it

We watch how you actually move and find where the restriction really lives: joint, muscle, or a body that is guarding. The answer is often not where the stiffness is felt.

What getting better looks like

Range returning a little at a time, movements feeling easier and more trustworthy, and simple habits to keep what you gain. A clear plan, adjusted with you as you improve.

Veiled photo band · 70% navy wash, homepage CTA treatment, at most once per page · requires this page's photo appended as the .veil CSS rule (see marked slot in the stylesheet) · run the photo overlay simulation before sign-off What it means for you

Movement you can trust again

Checking the blind spot without turning your whole body, reaching the top shelf, getting down to the grandchildren on the floor and back up again. Freedom of movement rarely returns all at once, but it does return, one easier movement at a time.

The right care for this · NEW condition-page element, the page's routing job per the IA · promote the one or two services this condition routes to, benefit-led, with direct links · keep to two cards maximum
The right care for this

Where this usually leads

Movement & Mobility Care

Made for exactly this: restoring comfortable, confident movement where it has stiffened or shrunk, gently and progressively.

Clinical Remedial Massage

When tight muscles and fascia are holding things back, focused hands-on work frees them so movement can return.

Safe, gentle, and at your pace

Everything is done with care and at a pace that suits you, and nothing is rushed or forced. The aim throughout is simple: that you feel in good hands, understood, and steadily better.

Recognised and accountable

Held to recognised professional standards

Members and registrants of the Royal Society of Medicine, the Complementary and Natural Healthcare Council, the Institute for Functional Medicine, the Society of Sports Therapists, the Institute of Sport and Remedial Massage and the British Medical Acupuncture Society, among others.
